metadata.name: Suspension #4
metadata.description:
Situated in an alternate reality where the general principles of physics do not apply, adjacent neighbourhoods are connected via cables, producing a city in suspension. The isometric building-like structures are inspired by architectural drawings and exploded engineering diagrams.

A comprehensive overview of the various parameters has been published as an article (https://www.fxhash.xyz/article/suspension), which is highly recommended reading. Importantly, the random number generator is seeded exclusively by the seed parameter. Since the hash has no bearing on the outcome, what you see is what you get when minting.

In live mode, add &size=<YOUR_PIXEL_SIZE> to export higher resolution images (Right click to save). For example, &size=4000 will set the width of the image to be 4000 pixels.

metadata.name: Windswept #134
metadata.description:
Windswept

Press 's' in live view to download a copy of your image.

You can add the URL parameter 'scale' to scale the size of the image for a higher resolution download (1600x2000 by default). For example, for a 3200x4000 image, add '&scale=2' to the end of the URL. 1, 2, 3, 4, and 5 are valid inputs. (Warning: large numbers may not work depending on your hardware/browser and can produce a very large download file of ~100mb)

teaboswell, 2022

ykxotkx

metadata.name: GPT-3 and me (free companion mint) #172
metadata.description: Free companion piece to my first Fx(text) article! I have never coded a single line of Javascript in my entire life, yet GPT-3 was able to write the code for this work via my inputs. To read the full article visit: https://www.fxhash.xyz/article/gpt-3-and-me:-a-generative-coding-study

metadata.name: Dithered Nostalgia #245
metadata.description:
Have you ever struck by a sudden surge of nostalgia?
Making you stop everything you did at that moment,
Trying hard to remember the memory behind it, and failed to do so?

Each of these lands hold a nostalgia unlocked,
Like how good nostalgia are, it’s dithered and full of holes.
Holes that can be filled with your own memories and interpretations.

• • •

The second collaboration of MEK.txt and IceRain in FXHash. This time using MEK.txt’s landscape pixelart series and its distinctive dithering textures, brilliantly translated into code by IceRain to pixel-perfect iterations.

Dithered Nostalgia is a step up from MEK.txt & IceRain’s first project (Bento Boxes), trying to make more sophisticated, more detailed and challenging things based on something they passionate about; landscape.

Controls:
[1] Set the resolution to Medium Resolution
[2] Set the resolution to High Resolution

metadata.name: Ordinary Places #161
metadata.description:
“Ordinary Places” is a fully-coded meditation on the poetic beauty of everyday landscapes. 

The architectural characters in these landscapes include water towers (inspired by Bernd & Hilla Becher’s “Water Tower” photography series), gas stations (inspired by Ed Ruscha’s “26 Gasoline Stations” photography book), and barns (inspired by childhood memories on my grandfather’s farm). 

Yet, while these scenes may seem ordinary upon first glance, something darker lurks beneath the surface. The hidden secrets of these Ordinary Places can only be revealed through the minting process. In this sense, the outputs are not merely a collection individual scenes, but instead, a coherent world animated by shared themes and narratives.     

Pressing the down arrow saves the coded output as a .png image.

metadata.name: Hillhouse #153
metadata.description:
On every important holiday of my childhood, I would return to my hometown, a village in a hilly area. The mist in the morning would have a psychedelic texture, like water ripples under the smudge of the deep blue sky. But at sunrise, the atmosphere becomes lively. All year round, spring, summer, autumn and winter, colourful and dreamlike.

The buildings in my hometown are white walls and black tiles. The fog in the village is very close to the ground, and the terrain is relatively undulating. I can often see rows of angular eaves and white walls penetrating the fog on other hillsides, like houses standing in the clouds. At that time, I always liked to climb up the hillside under the sunset after dinner, looking down at the village surrounded by clouds and mist after the rain. I could always stay at the top of the mountain and watch the clouds change from orange-red to blue. I didn't leave until the sky turned completely black. After I left the village, whenever I described such a scene with my friends, I would call such a building: Hillhouse.

I'm not in China now, and I don't know when I'll be able to see the misty landscape of my hometown again to create Hillhouse, a memorial to the village that inspired my aesthetics during my childhood.

I noticed many of my friends said after seeing my works that my townhouse is like a mountain. My original idea was to appropriately enlarge the proportion of the roof in the picture because I tried to balance the natural landscape and humanistic value in the work. Moreover, I also deliberately strengthened the corners and some obvious small pavilion buildings to make it different from the mountains. But, of course, art depends on personal understanding, so that's fine.


Mint at 188 tez, I will provide a 1/1 customised video NFT, physical printed work, and one WL spot for my following 2 projects on fxhash. You can choose your favourite palette and mountain style in our UI system and the soundtrack first. Then I will create a 20-sec video version NFT for you. The sound is originally created by @Dreminor. 

In terms of printed work, it will be printed on museum quality acid-free paper by giclee print technique. It will never fade 100 yrs. And the printed work will have my signature and seal on it. So yes I will give it to you with no charge, except the delivery fee requested from the delivery company.

I also considered secondary market transactions for collectors who minted at 188. I will offer a NEW customized video NFT and a NEW printed work to collectors who purchase the token minted in 188tez for more than 188tez on the secondary market. The video NFT is free but I will charge 100tez for the new printed work.

Mint at 138 tez, I will provide a printed work and one WL spot for my next project on fxhash.

Mint at 64 tez. I will provide one WL spot for my next project on fxhash.


Btw, the computational expense in this project is high, so if you cannot open live, please try to use a better computer to generate or use Visual Studio Code. If you cannot solve this problem, feel free to contact me, I would like to offer help.

Due to some reasons, the preview will be different from the generative artwork. So pls open live to have a look and it will be much better than preview.

This project is created by Arctic--Cc and Van.

metadata.name: Continuum #234
metadata.description:
This project is the culmination of many generative art experiments I have run throughout 2022. It relies heavily on the random walks through color and space techniques I have been working with, but also introduces other elements related to 2D and 3D rotation, framing and grains.

Continuum began as an abstract composition made up of a set of interacting elements against a background. This is still true. However, in my own mind, I have come to consider many Continuum pieces as peculiar, stylized observations of massive futuristic spacial, or quantum, constructions. Maybe even involving a wormhole through the fabric of space-time for some civilization well past 1 on the Kardashev scale. At the center is an erratic crystalline form, which is fed by power trains, and all around is a massive multicolored rotating containment structure.

Aside from the standard, inverse, threshold and washed types, the most defining trait of each composition is the “Back Style” which relates to a point of construction, activity or deconstruction of these structures. This is further refined by the panel style, the panel density and the rotation level. The other traits largely relate to color variability and aesthetics. 

This work has been coded using p5.js, and half of all royalties from the primary, and secondary, sales are assigned to the processing foundation.

Press s to download a 2400x2400 pixel image.

This work is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0 International License.

metadata.name: FXHASH Generative Logo #541
metadata.description:
Unique iteration of the first Generative Token minted on fxhash.

It also servers as the actual logo of the platform. It represents the generative processes driving fxhash tokens. Generative Tokens are fed with a unique hash to produce unique outputs based on the hash — they are functions with a hash as single input.

The growth that comes out of the text is driven by a "Dividing-Aggregating Walkers" (DAW) algorithm. The hash drives all the DAW settings, as well as a mutation factor which is responsible for changes in each Walker parameters as they divide.

Some iterations will produce very simple outputs whereas others might be more chaotic.

Piece by @ciphrd for fxhash

metadata.name: Sequence #302
metadata.description:
Sequence by Hevey (@HeveyArt).

This artwork was created using p5js.

metadata.name: Shapes on a Plane #178
metadata.description:
“Shapes on a Plane” explores imaginary structures and worlds through simple shapes. It’s intended for small-scale print.

As a kid I was obsessed with Lego, Duplo, wooden building blocks... anything I could make something with.

I’d spend hours building things, imagining new worlds, new machines, new concepts, then i’d take them apart and create a whole new idea to explore.

To me, this piece to me is a reminder of that time - many days spent exploring ideas with those simple objects - no care for viability, for marketability, for audiences or business. Just pure fun.

Without this time in my life, I doubt I’d have found my passion for creating in the way I do today.

metadata.name: Clew #347
metadata.description:
Thank you for adding me to your collection. Now that I’m a part of your tez fam, please open me in live view, enter ‘9’ or ‘10’, wait, press ‘P’, print me, put me on your wall, and enjoy.
__

Clew is a generative art collection designed for your wall.

When opening live view, you’ll be prompted to choose a render quality (1 for lowest; 10 for highest). The higher quality renders are for printing and zooming in to choose your own sub-composition or explore the details/depth. Higher quality images will naturally take longer to render but will be worth the wait. If you find a partial render particularly nice, you can press spacebar to pause (and unpause) the render. Save a PNG by pressing ‘P’ or a JPEG with ‘J’. Medium and high quality renders are optimized for Chrome desktop and may not function on other browsers or mobile devices.

The processing length varies considerably, depending on the assigned traits. Therefore, not all previews will reflect the final output.

created using p5js

Clew
abstractment, 2022

Generative art made in Processing 4.

André Oliveira Cebola, 2022.

metadata.name: Possible Ways #159
metadata.description:
This project is written in javascript without using any other libraries, and it is an endless real-time software animation.

The appearance of each token is initially determined by the hash, but the size and positions of its elements change randomly over time, creating an infinite performance.

While working on this project, I was inspired by the elusiveness of the moment, that cannot be saved or predicted. It can only be experienced.

metadata.name: #CPS1 Red Planet #76
metadata.description:
Color Pattern Series, Part One: Red Planet

The first work in the series. It is a landscape of an unknown planet, boiling under a red sun. It can be any color, but it will always aspire to be blood-red.

Everyone gets their own unique landscape. Also, in some cases, the planet can be flooded with neon. And quite rarely, instead of a part of the landscape, the whole planet will be shown.

metadata.name: Brosser #107
metadata.description:
Exploring generative brush strokes to simulate real life paintings. 

- A selection of 19 different palettes
- 4 different drawing styles
- Randomized "color swap" between the primary and secondary colors in each palette

Several other factors determine the output of a piece, including angles, depth, brush length, and brush weight, among others.

metadata.name: CA Vista #234
metadata.description:
Stephen Wolfram's cellular automata and Perlin noise come together to generate landscapes.

Press 'S' to download.

metadata.name: LOOPERS #18
metadata.description: Generative animated painting made from gestural lines traveling through time. Some are rare.

metadata.name: KooBoh #104
metadata.description:
KooBoh delves into the world of cubes and colors within an isometric grid framework.

Press s in live mode for a PNG download
Press (1-5) to change resolution ( 2000x2800 | 4000x5600 | 6000x8400 | 8000x11200 | 10000x14000 )
